---
title: "Results"
---

## Key findings (draft)
Add bullet points as you go.

## Figures
Add figures as you generate them. Example:

```{r}
# If using R, put code here (optional)
# This code is intended to be used in the creation of our log scale boxplot and a presence v. absence bar chart

# libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(scales)

# read data
tma_diet <- read_csv("tma_df_with_diet.csv")

# create a log-safe column
tma_diet <- tma_diet %>%
  mutate(TMA_Log = TMA_Abundance + 1)

# create the box plot
ggplot(tma_diet, aes(x = Diet, y = TMA_Log, fill = Diet)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = comma_format()) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Vegetarian" = "seagreen", "Omnivore" = "steelblue")) +
  labs(
    title = "TMA-Associated Bacteria Abundance by Diet",
    x = "Diet Group",
    y = "TMA Abundance (log scale)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

# export & save boxplot
ggsave("tma_boxplot_log.jpeg", width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# create presence/absence bar chart 

# calculate counts + percentages manually
bar_data <- tma_diet %>%
  count(Diet, Present) %>%
  group_by(Diet) %>%
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n))

# plot
bar_plot <- ggplot(bar_data, aes(x = Diet, y = n, fill = Present)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = percent(percent, accuracy = 0.1)),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size = 4
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Present" = "palevioletred", "Absent" = "thistle")) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 36),
    breaks = seq(0, 35, by = 5)
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Presence of TMA-Associated Bacteria by Diet",
    x = "Diet Group",
    y = "Number of Samples",
    fill = "TMA Detection"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

# export and save bar chart
ggsave("tma_presence_bar.jpeg", width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)


# If using Python, put code here (optional)
